reasoning: Confirmed. Lines 1214-1215 read pageSize and pageNumber from $_GET and apply sanitize_text_field(), which strips HTML but does not coerce to integer or enforce positive bounds. Line 1216 uses these in arithmetic ($offset = ($pageNumber - 1) * $pageSize) and lines 1217-1219 pass $pageSize directly as the 'number' argument to get_sites(). WordPress WP_Site_Query coerces the 'number' argument to int; passing '0' (which sanitize_text_field passes through unchanged) causes get_sites() to fetch all subsites with no limit, enabling resource exhaustion on large networks. No absint/intval/cast guard is present anywhere in the path. Auth is correctly stated: nonce verification (wp_verify_nonce) fires first at line 1199, then a capability check at line 1207 requiring manage_network_plugins (multisite) or manage_options (single-site) — both are administrator-level. The attack requires a valid nonce obtainable from the settings page by an authenticated admin. Severity is low because the exploitable user must be a network super-admin or site admin, making this an authenticated DoS-only vector, not a privilege escalation or data exfiltration path.
